title: cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koraiana
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koraiana, also known as the Japanese plum yew, is a plant species within the Cephalotaxaceae family, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and red berries. The plant contains taxine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Research suggests that the health benefits of Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koraiana include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions are commonly used to harness the plant's medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
